program_outcomes: Engineering Knowledge: Knowledge of mathematics, science, fundamental engineering, computational methods, and topics specific to the Software Engineering discipline; the ability to apply this knowledge to solve complex engineering problems.

Problem Analysis: The ability to identify, formulate, and analyze complex engineering problems using knowledge of basic sciences, mathematics, and engineering, while considering the relevant UN Sustainable Development Goals* related to the problem at hand.

Engineering Design: The ability to design creative solutions for complex engineering problems; the ability to design complex systems, processes, devices, or products to meet current and future requirements by taking into account realistic constraints and conditions.

Use of Techniques and Tools: The ability to select and use appropriate techniques, resources, and modern engineering and IT tools, including prediction and modeling, for the analysis and solution of complex engineering problems, with an awareness of their limitations.

Research and Investigation: The ability to use research methods, including literature review, experimental design, conducting experiments, data collection, and analysis and interpretation of results, for the investigation of complex engineering problems.

Global Impact of Engineering Practices: Knowledge of the societal, health and safety, economic, sustainability, and environmental impacts of engineering practices within the framework of the UN Sustainable Development Goals*; awareness of the legal implications of engineering solutions.

Ethical Conduct: Acting in accordance with the principles of professional engineering ethics*; awareness of ethical responsibilities, impartial conduct without any form of discrimination, and inclusivity of diversity.
Individual and Team Work: The ability to work effectively both individually and as a member or leader in disciplinary and multidisciplinary teams (face-to-face, remote, or hybrid).

Oral and Written Communication: The ability to communicate effectively on technical subjects in oral and written forms, considering the diverse backgrounds (such as education, language, profession) of the target audience.
Project Management: Knowledge of professional practices such as project management and economic feasibility analysis; awareness of entrepreneurship and innovation.
Lifelong Learning: The ability for independent and continuous learning; the ability to adapt to new and emerging technologies and to think critically in the face of technological change.

courses.name: Algorithms and Programming I
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach students computer programming using the C programming language while emphasizing problem-solving methods and software engineering principles. It also introduces algorithm design and development through pseudo-code and flowchart techniques.
courses.description: Hardware, software and algorithm definitions, algorithm characteristics, algorithm writing rules, flow charts, basic elements of C language, I/O, control structures, functions, basic data types, arrays and pointers.
learning_outcomes: Analyzes complex problems to develop systematic solution methods; conducts pre-software design processes using flowcharts and pseudo-code tools.

Effectively utilizes data types, arithmetic operators, and precedence rules in C; implements selection (if-else, switch-case) and repetition (for, while, do-while) control structures to manage program flow.
Organizes data in memory using single and multi-dimensional arrays; performs sequential access and manipulation on data sets using loop structures.

Decomposes problems into modular components via library and user-defined functions; applies the fundamentals of memory management through parameter passing (pass-by-value and pass-by-reference), variable scope, and the concept of pointers.

Implements theoretically designed algorithms by coding them in the C programming language; identifies and resolves logical and syntactical errors during the program execution process through systematic debugging techniques.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Overview of Computers and Programming (Introduction to computer hardware and software, introduction to software development methodology)

Hafta 2: Introduction to problem solving and the concept of algorithm (analyzing the problem, designing a solution, implementing the design, testing, maintaining, introduction to fundamentals of algorithmic problem solving). Algorithms (formal definition of an algorithm, algorithm representation, pseudocode, flowcharts).

Hafta 3: Overview of C Programming Language (general form of a C program, language elements, variable declarations and data types, arithmetic expression and precedence rules, formatting numbers in program output)

Hafta 4: Simple Data Types (Integer and character variables and expressions, floating point numbers and variables, distinction between integers and floating point numbers, floating point arithmetic, double type, type casting)
Hafta 5: Selection structures (control structures, conditions, the if statement, if statement with compound statements, decision steps in algorithms)
Hafta 6: Selection structures (nested if statements and multiple alternative decisions, the switch statement)
Hafta 7: Repetition and loop structures (repetition in programs, counting loops and the for loop, conditional loops and the while loop)
Hafta 8: Repetition and loop structures (the do-while loop, sentinel/flag controlled loops, loop design, nested loops)
Hafta 9: Arrays (declaring and referencing arrays, array subscripts, using for loops for sequential access)
Hafta 11: Designing with functions (functions without parameters) and functions of libraries
Hafta 12: Programming with functions (functions with simple I/O parameters), scope of variables
Hafta 13: Introduction to pointer concept (pass by value, pass by reference, arrays as function argument)

courses.name: Calculus I
courses.purpose: The aim of the course is to teach the basic concepts and theorems of differentiation and integration calculation, to show how the problems in different fields such as science and engineering are solved with these concepts and theorems. The object of the course is to make the students realize the importance of differentiation and integration calculation, examine and solve the problems under the light of these theorems.
courses.description: Functions, graphs of functions, limits and continuity, derivatives, differentiation rules, applications of derivatives, definite-indefinite integration, techniques and applications of definite integrals.
learning_outcomes: Know the concepts of limits and continuity of functions of a single variable and performs related calculations.
Know the concept of derivative and some of its applications and performs related calculations.
Know the concepts of definite, indefinite and improper integrals and some of their applications and performs related calculations.
Know functions, types of functions, and learn their domains and ranges
Learn the Mean Value Theorems, and solve extremum problems
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Functions and Their Graphs; Combining Functions; Shifting and Scaling Graphs, Trigonometric Functions; Exponential Functions; Inverse Functions and Logarithms.
Hafta 2: Limit of a Function and Limit Laws; One-Sided Limits.
Hafta 3: Continuity; Limits Involving Infinity; Asymptotes of Graphs.
Hafta 4: Tangents and the Derivative at a Point; The Derivative as a Function; Differentiation Rules; The Derivative as a Rate of Change.
Hafta 5: Derivatives of Trigonometric Functions; The Chain Rule; Implicit Differentiation.
Hafta 6: Derivatives of Inverse Functions and Logarithms; Inverse Trigonometric Functions.
Hafta 7: Linearization and Differentials; Extreme Values of Functions; The Mean Value Theorem.
Hafta 8: Monotonic Functions and the First Derivative Test; Concavity and Curve Sketching.
Hafta 9: Indeterminate Forms and L'Hopital's Rule; Antiderivatives; Sigma Notation and Limits of Finite Sums.
Hafta 10: The Definite Integral; The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus; Indefinite Integrals and the Substitution Method.
Hafta 11: Substitution and Area Between Curves
Hafta 14: Integration of Rational Functions by Partial Fractions; Improper Integrals.

courses.name: Physics I
courses.purpose: To introduce the fundamental concepts of motion necessary for engineering science and provide essential background for engineering students. To provide students with a deeper understanding of fundamental laws and concepts of natural phenomena. To improve students problem solving skills. To strengthen students creative and systematic thinking capability.
courses.description: Physics and Measurement, Physical Quantities, Motion in One Dimension, Vectors, Motion in two dimensions, Newton's Laws of Motion, Work and Kinetic Energy, Potential Energy and Conservation of Mechanical Energy, Linear Momentum, Conservation of Linear Momentum, Collisions, Dynamics of a System of Particles, Rotational Dynamics, Torque and Angular Momentum, Equilibrium of Rigid Bodies.
learning_outcomes: Defines physical quantities, units, and dimensional analysis; distinguishes between scalar and vector quantities and applies vector operations in multi-dimensional space.
Analyzes the kinematic equations of motion in one and two dimensions (linear, projectile, and circular motion); solves force interactions on particles and systems using Newton?s Laws of Motion.

Explains the work-energy theorem along with the concepts of kinetic and potential energy; applies the principles of conservation of mechanical energy and linear momentum to collisions and similar physical phenomena in isolated systems.
Analyzes the rotational motion of rigid bodies about a fixed axis within the framework of torque and moment of inertia; solves problems related to rotational kinematics and dynamics.
Performs experiments related to the course topics, analyzes the results, and prepares reports.

Hafta 4: Description of Motion in Two Dimensions
Hafta 7: Circular Motion and Other Applications of Newton's Law
Hafta 8: Circular Motion and Other Applications of Newton's Law (Continuation)
Hafta 9: Work, Energy and Power ,Conservation of Energy
Hafta 10: Work, Energy and Power ,Conservation of Energy (Continuation)

Hafta 12: System of Particles, Linear Momentum and Collisions, Conservation Laws (Continuation)
Hafta 13: Rotation of a Rigid Object about a Fixed Axis.
Hafta 14: Rotation of a Rigid Object about a Fixed Axis (Continuation)

courses.name: Introduction to Software Engineering
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to teach the basic concepts of computer systems and software engineering.
courses.description: Binary systems, hardware layer, programming layer, operating systems layer, applications layer, communications layer, requirements engineering, writing a program, software engineering problems, software process models, new and emerging process methodologies, testing, and maintenance.
learning_outcomes: understanding of the layered and modular nature of computer systems
identify the principal components of a given computer system
analyse a problem and identify functional, non-functional requirements
identify software engineering problems, including design, implementation, and testing of software systems
understanding of software process models

Hafta 13: New and Emerging Process Methodologies

courses.name: Turkish Language I
courses.purpose: To show the features and functions of Turkish language by giving examples; to develop students' oral and written expression skills and habits for conveying their feelings, thoughts, plans, impressions, observations, and experiences directly and effectively; to improve students' vocabulary through written and oral texts; and to teach the rules for accurate understanding of texts they read and programs they listen to.
courses.description: This course offers instruction in understanding, explanation, reading and writing. Various features of written expression are presented and examined critically. Punctuation marks, writing rules, and the correct and effective use of these rules in written and oral expressions are covered.
learning_outcomes: Recall and repeat the features and functions of Turkish.
Repeat the features of spelling and punctuation and adapt them to their writings and speeches.
Distinguish the basic concepts of language and explain the corporate relations.
Analyze and restructure reading-listening-monitoring and comprehension.
Analyze and restructure their expression skills (written-oral).
Recall the historical background of Turkish and discuss current problems of it.

courses.name: Algorithms and Programming II
courses.purpose: This course serves as an advanced continuation of CEN133. It aims to provide students with an in-depth understanding of advanced programming techniques and algorithms using the C programming language, while strengthening problem-solving skills through practical programming scenarios.
courses.description: Strings, recursion, struct and union types, text and binary file processing, user-defined libraries, pointers, and dynamic memory allocation.
learning_outcomes: Develop efficient algorithms using recursion, pointers, and data structures to solve complex computational problems.
Utilize dynamic memory allocation and function pointers to write memory-efficient programs and optimize performance.
Design and implement structured programs using structures, unions, and enums.
Develop sustainable software applications and basic database-like systems using file handling (text/binary), modular programming, and preprocessor directives.
Perform applications related to the course topics, analyze the results, and prepare reports.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Pointers: Introduction to pointers, pointer arithmetic, memory addresses, and dynamic memory allocation using malloc, calloc, realloc, and free.
Hafta 2: Unions, Enums, and Function Pointers: Defining and using unions, and enums, followed by an introduction to function pointers.
Hafta 3: Strings in C - Part I: Fundamental string operations, including declaration, initialization, standard library functions (strlen, strcpy, strcat, strcmp), and arrays of strings.

Hafta 4: Strings in C - Part II: Advanced string processing techniques such as character operations, string-to-number and number-to-string conversions, and implementing simple text manipulation programs.
Hafta 6: Recursion - Fundamentals: Understanding recursion through function tracing, recursive mathematical functions, and recursive functions with array and string parameters.
Hafta 7: Recursion - Problem Solving: Applying recursion to real-world problems, including sorting algorithms, tree traversal, and solving complex computational problems.
Hafta 9: Linked Lists: Introduction to singly and doubly linked lists, including insertion, deletion, and traversal operations.
Hafta 10: Text and Binary Files - Part I: Basics of file operations, including opening, reading, writing, and closing text and binary files.
Hafta 12: Text and Binary Files - Part II: Implementing searching, sorting, and modifying records in files, leading to a simple file-based database system.
Hafta 13: Modular Programming in C: Creating and managing header files, implementation files, and understanding storage classes for structured program development.

Hafta 14: Preprocessor Directives and Macro Definitions: Exploring preprocessor directives (#define, #ifdef, #ifndef), conditional compilation, defining macros with parameters, and passing arguments to main.

courses.purpose: The purpose of this course is to introduce the students to more advanced topics in calculus and to develop the ability to solve and model complex mathematical problems.

Learn parametric curves and polar coordinates and perform the related calculations.
Understand vectors in three-dimensional coordinate systems, including the dot product and cross product, and write the equations of lines and planes.
Know limits, continuity, and partial derivatives of multivariable functions and perform the related calculations.

learning_outcomes: Solve the systems of linear equations, provide arithmetic operations with matrices, compute the inverse of a matrix.
Determine the value of determinant of a matrix, use Cramer rule to solve linear systems.
Learn the concepts of vector space, basis, dimension and rank.
Evaluate the eigenvalues and the corresponding eigenvectors of the matrix.

courses.name: Turkish Language II
courses.purpose: The course aims to show students the features and rules of the Turkish language with examples; to develop their ability and habit of expressing feelings, thoughts, designs, impressions, observations, and experiences accurately and effectively in speech and writing; to improve vocabulary through written and spoken texts; to teach how to understand texts read or programs listened to accurately; and to strengthen language skills fundamental to communication. It also aims to help students understand academic language and writing and improve their ability to write and discuss academic texts.
courses.description: The course covers the features of academic language and writing, the use of definitions, concepts and terms in academic writings, objective and subjective expression, the structure and types of academic texts such as articles, reports and scientific abstracts, claiming and proposing writing, the formal features of scientific reports and articles, report writing steps, explaining, discussing and establishing relationships between texts, citing references, creating bibliographies, writing titles, summarizing, keyword writing, ethical principles in scientific writing, and academic text writing practices. It also includes text, techniques of expression, ways of developing thought, text types, artistic texts, thought articles, verbal expression types, official correspondence, spelling and punctuation rules, sentence information, and expression disorders.
learning_outcomes: Read and understand a text written in Turkish and understand the narrative technique used in the text, and identify ways to improve thinking.
Determine the characteristics of texts by understanding the differences between texts.
Explain the similarities and differences between thought writings.
Classify and exemplify types of verbal expression.
Comprehend expression disorders at the word and sentence levels.

Know the characteristics of academic language and writing, benefit from definitions, concepts and terms in academic writings, master scientific ethics rules, and prepare academic texts in light of this information.

Hafta 2: Unprepared speaking practices, written and oral composition exercises, plan in written expression
Hafta 3: Paragraph, narration techniques (explanatory, storyteller, etc.), ways to improve thought (definition, showing example etc.)
Hafta 4: Types of text (artistic-instructional texts), examination of text samples
Hafta 5: Artistic writings (story, novel, poetry, theater)
Hafta 6: Thought writing (interview, interview, travel writing, memory, daily, life story, self life story)
Hafta 7: Thought articles (article, clause, criticism, essay)
Hafta 9: Oral expression types-discussion based expression types (panel, forum, open session, discussion, debate)
Hafta 10: Oral expression types - discussion-based expression types (symposium, interview, conference, congress, discourse)
Hafta 11: Official correspondence (CV, report, record, business letter, e-mail, petition), official correspondence studies
Hafta 12: Access to information resources and library use, scientific ethical principles, style in scientific research, quoting and citing
Hafta 13: Features of academic language and writing, definition, concepts and rules in academic writing
Hafta 14: The effects of mass media on Turkish, expression disorders, the use of language in mass media and applications related to expression disorders

courses.name: Principles of Atatürk and History of Turkish Revolution I
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to help learners understand the meaning and importance of the Turkish Revolution. It also aims to teach the collapse of the Ottoman Empire, the reasons for the Turkish Independence War, the preparations for the National Struggle, and information about the Lausanne Treaty, which is the foundational treaty of the Turkish Republic.
courses.description: General concepts about the course, the collapse of the Ottoman Empire, preparations made for the National Struggle, the opening of the Turkish Grand National Assembly, the Mudanya Armistice, and the Lausanne Treaty.
learning_outcomes: Be able to know the collapse reasons of the Ottoman Empire and the process conditions of the Turkish National Struggle.
Ability to compare the conditions of the past and those of our days by learning Turkish Independence War from the beginning up to now.
Be able to have opinion about the problems of the country under the light of the lessons obtained from the past.
Ability to create bridge between past and the future.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: A general introduction to the course, concepts like revolution, confusion, etc.
Hafta 2: Collapse reasons of the Ottoman Empire.
Hafta 3: Reasons leading to National Struggle and Mudros Armistice.
Hafta 4: Stepping of Mustafa Kemal Pasha on Samsun and overview the situation.
Hafta 5: Preparations for the congresses which are the first step for National Struggle.
Hafta 6: Erzurum and Sivas Congresses and the decisions of these congresses.
Hafta 7: Opening of Turkish Great National Assembly in Ankara and signing Sevres Treaty.
Hafta 8: National Struggle until Sakarya Battle, I. and II. İnönü Battles.
Hafta 9: Sakarya Battle, Başkomutanlık Piched Battle and the Great Attack.
Hafta 11: Preparations for Lausanne Conference and collecting of the conference.
Hafta 12: Lausanne discussions, issues and giving discussions for a while.

courses.name: Computer Networks
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a comprehensive understanding of the Internet's layered architecture using a top-down approach. Students will learn the fundamental principles of the application, transport, network, data link, and physical layers, and understand how core Internet protocols operate. The course develops the ability to analyze network performance in terms of delay, loss, and throughput, and introduces routing, addressing, wired and wireless networking, and network security concepts.
courses.description: This course examines the fundamental principles of computer networking using a top-down approach. After introducing the structure of the Internet and network performance metrics, application layer protocols such as HTTP, DNS, and e-mail are studied along with basic socket programming and web applications. At the transport layer, UDP and TCP protocols, reliable data transfer, flow control, and congestion control mechanisms are analyzed. The network layer covers packet switching, routing algorithms, IP addressing, and subnetting. In the data link layer, framing, error detection, multiple access protocols, and Ethernet are discussed. Fundamental principles of wireless and mobile networks, including Wi-Fi and cellular architectures, are introduced. Finally, basic concepts of network security, including encryption, authentication, and secure communication protocols, are presented.
learning_outcomes: Explains the layered architecture of the Internet and the fundamental functions of each layer.
Analyzes the operating principles of application layer protocols such as HTTP, DNS, and SMTP.
Compares TCP and UDP features and determines appropriate usage scenarios.
Calculates and interprets delay, loss, and throughput in packet-switched networks.
Explains IP addressing, subnetting, and routing concepts.
Explains the fundamental logic of routing algorithms such as Dijkstra and Distance Vector.
Describes error detection, framing, and multiple access methods in the data link layer.
Explains the fundamental characteristics of wireless networks and the concept of mobility.
Describes fundamental concepts of network security such as encryption, authentication, and integrity.
Interprets the interaction of network protocols in the context of end-to-end data communication.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to computer networks, Internet structure, network edge and core, packet switching
Hafta 2: Network performance: delay, loss, throughput; protocol layers and Internet architecture
Hafta 3: Application layer: principles of network applications, client-server and P2P architectures
Hafta 4: Web and HTTP protocol, web caching, cookies
Hafta 5: DNS system, e-mail architecture and protocols (SMTP, POP3, IMAP)
Hafta 6: Introduction to transport layer, UDP and principles of reliable data transfer
Hafta 7: TCP protocol, flow control, congestion control
Hafta 8: Network layer: routing principles, routing algorithms
Hafta 9: IP addressing, subnetting, IPv4 and IPv6
Hafta 10: Data link layer: error detection, framing, multiple access protocols
Hafta 12: Ethernet, switching, local area networks
Hafta 13: Wireless and mobile networks, Wi-Fi, cellular networks
Hafta 14: Network security: basics of encryption, authentication, secure communication
resources: Kurose, J. F., & Ross, K. W. (2020). Computer Networking: A Top-Down Approach (8th ed.). Pearson.

courses.name: Occupational Health and Safety I
courses.purpose: The primary objective of this course is to ensure that engineering students develop a strong understanding of occupational health and safety (OHS) principles. The course covers the rules that graduates who will serve as engineers are required to follow in the workplace, the importance of responsible behavior, and the preventive measures that must be taken to avoid accidents before they occur. Students also receive fundamental knowledge about the procedures to follow in the event of a workplace accident.
courses.description: This course introduces fundamental concepts and topics in occupational health and safety. It develops a conceptual framework for working with worker communities and applying public health principles. Work-related hazards are identified in terms of recognition and control, and the course integrates knowledge from behavioral sciences, industrial hygiene, injury epidemiology, safety, toxicology, and epidemiology within the context of program development and management frameworks. Occupational health and safety practices in Turkey and around the world are also examined and compared, with emphasis on current legislation and regulations in Turkey.
learning_outcomes: Recognize the interrelatedness of public health, management, employees, and the government to the goals of occupational health and safety.
Demonstrate a base of knowledge in the recognition and assessment of health and safety hazards in the workplace.
Identify a conceptual framework for the practice of occupational health and safety.
Relate health promotion/prevention/protection concepts to the occupational health and safety program.
Discuss the roles and functions of the occupational health and safety professional in the application of the conceptual framework.
Apply theories and concepts of occupational health and safety to the development and management of programs.
Identify education, engineering, and enforcement controls for the prevention of occupational health and safety problems.
Knows the relevant legislation.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Course Outline: Introduction, Evolution of Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) Concepts and Regulations
Hafta 2: 01. Development of ISG Concepts and Rules and Overview

courses.name: Mobile Robot Design
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to recognize robotic parts and concepts, and to program control electronics with these parts for a specific purpose.
courses.description: Robot concept, mobile robots, mechanical properties of robots, robotic motors, motor drivers, control electronics (built-in boards), sensor types, general structure of robot programming languages.
learning_outcomes: Defines robot systems.
Develops knowledge of various robot structures and workspaces.
Learns about the issues related to the operation of robotic systems.
Defines the mechanical properties of robot types.
Recognizes motors and sensors.
Gains some knowledge and skills about robot control.
It uses robot programming languages.

courses.name: Internet of Things Technologies
courses.purpose: The primary goal of this course is to provide students with a foundational understanding of the Internet of Things (IoT) by exploring its components, technologies, and applications. Students will learn to design, develop, and analyze IoT systems, emphasizing real-world problem-solving, data analytics, and security considerations.
courses.description: This course covers the fundamental concepts, components, and applications of the Internet of Things (IoT). Topics include IoT architecture, communication protocols, hardware platforms, data analytics, and security considerations. Students will gain hands-on experience through projects involving IoT device setup, data transmission, cloud integration, and practical applications.
learning_outcomes: Understand the fundamental concepts and architecture of IoT systems.
Identify and utilize IoT components such as sensors, actuators, and communication protocols.
Design and develop basic IoT applications using hardware and software platforms like Raspberry Pi and Arduino.
Implement secure and scalable IoT solutions by applying best practices in security and data management.
Analyze IoT-generated data using analytics techniques and tools.
Explore real-world IoT applications and assess their societal and industrial impact.
Apply IoT concepts in practical projects to solve real-world problems.

Hafta 7: IoT Data Analytics and Machine Learning Applications

courses.name: Artificial Neural Networks
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach students the fundamental principles of artificial neural networks and help them understand their applications in various fields. It also provides mathematical foundations and practical experience with different network architectures to solve real-world problems.
courses.description: This course covers the fundamental concepts and applications of artificial neural networks. Students learn the neuron model, feedforward networks, the backpropagation algorithm, activation functions, optimization techniques, deep learning architectures such as CNNs and RNNs, and their real-world applications. The course also addresses regularization, transfer learning, and ethical considerations.
learning_outcomes: Be able to explain the fundamental components and working principles of artificial neural networks.
Identify and implement various neural network architectures (MLP, CNN, RNN).
Train neural networks using backpropagation and optimization techniques.
Improve neural networks with regularization techniques to address overfitting and underfitting.
Apply neural networks to different types of data such as images, text, and time series.
Understand the potential of neural networks in various applications and manage projects in those areas.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Introduction to Artificial Neural Networks (ANNs)
Hafta 4: Activation Functions and Forward Propagation
Hafta 10: Advanced Topics: Autoencoders and Generative Models
Hafta 11: Applications of Neural Networks, Ethics, and Future Outlook
resources: Goodfellow, I., Bengio, Y., Courville, A. (2016). Deep Learning. MIT Press.

courses.name: Wireless Network
courses.purpose: This course provides a broad survey of wireless communications, with in-depth coverage of protocols, transmission methods, and IEEE 802.11 standards.
courses.description: Overview of wireless communications and networking, including probability, statistics, traffic theories, Fourier analysis, radio frequency fundamentals, antennas, mobile radio propagation, characterization of the wireless channel, signal encoding techniques, spread spectrum, coding and error control, satellite communications, cellular wireless networks, mobile IP and wireless access protocol, wireless LAN technology, IEEE 802.11 wireless LAN standard, Bluetooth, ad hoc networks, and wireless sensor networks.
learning_outcomes: Students will learn RF communication and antenna design aspects of the technology.
Students will learn how wireless systems work, how mobility is supported, how infrastructure underlies such systems, and what interactions are needed among different functional components.
Students will appreciate the usefulness and applications of mathematical formulations in numerous wireless and mobile systems.
Students will appreciate the use of wireless and mobile communications in areas such as commerce, education, and defense.

Students will be able to evaluate common issues in a commercial system such as range, number of distribution infrastructure access points, number of users for each access point, appropriate coordination of channels between access points, and channel bandwidth requirements.
Students will learn about various wireless infrastructures such as satellite systems, cellular systems, LANs, and PANs.
Students will learn about wireless data communications topics such as signal encoding techniques, spread spectrum, coding, and error control.
Students should be encouraged to use one of the simulators (ns, OPNET, or other stable simulators) to get a feel for the overall system complexity.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Overview of Wireless Communications and Networking
Hafta 2: Probability, Statistics, and Traffic Theories, Fourier Analysis
Hafta 4: Mobile Radio Propagation, Characterization of the Wireless Channel
Hafta 10: Mobile IP and Wireless Access Protocol
Hafta 14: Ad Hoc Networks, Wireless Sensor Networks

courses.name: Digital Signal Processing
courses.purpose: This course introduces fundamental principles of digital signal processing.
courses.description: The course covers introduction to signals and systems, complex algebra review, discrete-time signals and systems in time and frequency domains, sampling, Z-transform, digital filter design, and Fast Fourier Transform.
learning_outcomes: Explain the fundamental concepts of a digital signal processing system.
Develop an analytical framework for problem analysis and solution.
Use basic tools to analyze discrete-time signals in time and frequency domains.
Use basic tools to analyze discrete-time systems in time and frequency domains.
Design and implement digital filters.

courses.name: Energy Storage Systems
courses.purpose: This course aims to give insight into the principles and applications of energy storage systems by providing general, experimental, and analytical knowledge on energy storage systems.
courses.description: Energy terminology, energy types and how to store energy, energy storage types and systems, test procedures, and application fields of energy storage.
learning_outcomes: They have knowledge on concept of energy and energy storage.
They have knowledge on energy types.
They identify energy storage systems.
They get the hang of necessity of energy storage and applications.
They get understanding with their presentation homework and practise after laboratory.

courses.name: Wireless and Mobile Networks
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with knowledge of the architectures, basic operating principles, standards, and applications of wireless and mobile networks, and to develop their problem-solving skills in these areas.
courses.description: Definition, types, generations, advantages, limitations, and security problems of wireless and mobile networks; transmission fundamentals, analog and digital signals, channel capacity, transmission media, and multiplexing; wireless communication technology, antennas and propagation, line-of-sight transmission; signal encoding techniques, spread spectrum techniques; wireless networking, satellite communications, cellular wireless networks, cordless systems and wireless local loop, mobile IP, wireless LANs, wireless LAN technology, and IEEE 802.11 wireless LAN standards.
learning_outcomes: Design cellular network layouts and analyze system capacity using Trunking Theory to meet specific Grade of Service and coverage requirements.
Evaluate the impact of large- and small-scale radio propagation impairments on link performance to calculate comprehensive link budgets.
Compare multiple access techniques regarding spectral efficiency and interference, and explain the physical resource structures of modern LTE/5G systems.
Describe the mobility management network architecture and assess the performance challenges of running standard TCP/IP protocols over lossy wireless links.
Analyze the architectural evolution toward 5G and evaluate the system requirements for emerging wireless technologies like Massive IoT and Integrated Sensing and Communications.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Mobile Systems: History (1G-5G), The "System" view vs. "Link" view. Prerequisite Primer: Review of Decibels (dB, dBm) and basic signal power concepts.
Hafta 2: The Cellular Concept: Frequency Reuse, Hexagonal Geometry, Cluster Size (N), Co-channel Interference (S/I). Prerequisite Primer: Basic Geometry and Distance formulas.

Hafta 3: Traffic Engineering (Trunking Theory): Grade of Service (GoS), Erlang B & C Formulas, Cell Splitting, Sectoring. Prerequisite Primer: Intro to Queuing Theory, Poisson Processes, and probability of "blocking."
Hafta 4: Mobile Propagation (Large Scale): Path Loss Models (Free Space, Hata, Okumura), Log-normal Shadowing. Prerequisite Primer: Review of Logarithms, Random Variables, and Standard Deviation.

Hafta 5: Mobile Propagation (Small Scale Fading): Multipath Fading, Doppler Spread, Coherence Time/Bandwidth. Prerequisite Primer: Critical Review of PDFs, CDFs, Gaussian vs. Rayleigh distributions, Convolution.
Hafta 6: Multiple Access - Part I (Legacy): FDMA, TDMA, CDMA (DSSS basics, Near-Far Problem). Prerequisite Primer: Orthogonality concepts, Autocorrelation.

Hafta 7: Multiple Access - Part II (Modern) & RECITATION 1: Lecture (1x40m): OFDMA (The LTE/5G Standard), Cyclic Prefix. Recitation (2x40m): Review of Weeks 1-6 (Cellular Design, Erlang Calc, Path Loss).
Hafta 8: MIDTERM EXAM #1: No Lecture. Covers Weeks 1-7.
Hafta 9: The Mobile MAC Layer: Hidden/Exposed Terminal Problems, SDMA, Aloha vs. CSMA/CA. Prerequisite Primer: Basic Packet Switching logic (collisions).
Hafta 10: Mobility Management (Network Layer): Network Architecture (LTE EPC), HSS/MME, Handover Algorithms, Tracking Areas.
Hafta 11: Modern PHY (MIMO): Diversity, Spatial Multiplexing, Beamforming, Massive MIMO. Prerequisite Primer: Matrix basics (Qualitative), Spatial concepts.
Hafta 12: Mobile Transport/IP & RECITATION 2: Lecture (1x40m): Mobile IP (Tunneling), TCP over Wireless (Snooping/Indirect). Recitation (2x40m): Review of Weeks 9-11 (MAC, Handovers, MIMO).
Hafta 13: MIDTERM EXAM #2: No Lecture. Covers Weeks 9-12.
Hafta 14: Future Trends & FINAL RECITATION: Lecture (1x40m): 5G Architecture, mMTC (IoT), 6G Visions. Recitation (2x40m): Comprehensive Final Exam Preparation.
resources: Rappaport, T. S. 2002. Wireless communications: principles and practice. Prentice Hall PTR. Upper Saddle River, NJ. ISBN: 0130422320.
Molisch, A. F. 2022. Wireless Communications: From Fundamentals to Beyond 5G, 3rd Edition. Wiley-IEEE Press. ISBN: 978-1-119-11721-6.
Schiller, J. H. 2003. Mobile Communications, 2nd Edition. Addison-Wesley. ISBN: 978-8131724262.

courses.name: Communication Networks
courses.purpose: In this course, it is aimed that students gain the knowledge of classifications, architectures, operation principles, standards, technologies, and applications of computer networks and data communication services and have the ability of problem solving on these subjects.
courses.description: Definition, uses and classification of computer Networks; Local Area Networks (LANs), Metropolitan Area Networks (MANs), Wide Area Networks (WANs), wireless networks, and internetworks; network architecture, layered network structure, interface and services; OSI and TCP/IP models, network examples, network standardization; data communication services, SMDS, X.25, FR, ISDN, ATM, BISDN; physical layer, RS-232, RS-449, X.21, ISDN and V.35 interfaces, types of transmission medium; data link layer, functions of data link layer, framing, flow control, error control, HDLC, SLIP and PPP protocols; medium access control sublayer, channel allocation methods used in broadcast networks; network devices, repeaters, hubs, bridges, routers and routing algorithms, LAN switches, Layer-3 switches and gateways.
learning_outcomes: Classify computer networks.
Define topologies and operation principles of LANs, MANs and WANs.
Formulate the relationship of data transmission, frame length and the network cable length.

Define the layered network structure and the related concepts; define the functions of the layers for the OSI model and the TCP/IP model; define the interface and service concepts, service primitives, connection-oriented and connectionless services, reliable and non-reliable services.
Define the circuit-switched and packet-switched networks and distinguish their properties.
Discuss the advantages of network standardization and recognize the network standardization organizations.

Calculate information contents, data transmission rate, bit error rate, transfer rate of information bits, and channel capacity; define serial, parallel, synchronous, and asynchronous transmission, and distinguish their properties in data communication.
Define architectures and operation principles of the following data communication services: SMDS, X.25, FR, ISDN, BISDN, ATM; and distinguish their properties.
Define the basic properties of the following physical layer protocols: RS-232, RS-449, X.21, ISDN, and V.35; distinguish their properties.
Define the functions and services of the data link layer; describe framing and flow control methods.
Describes error control methods; calculates CRC and Hamming codes in source and destination computers.
Define the basic functions, architectures, and operation principles of repeaters, passive hubs, bridges, routers, layer 2 switches, layer 3 switches, and gateways.

Define basic routing algorithms: Distinguish the differences of static and dynamic routing algorithms; describe the operation principles of Dijkstra, Bellman-Ford, flooding, distance vector, link-state routing algorithms; obtains the routing table of a router in a given network by using routing algorithms.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Presentation of the course. Definition, uses and classification of computer networks; local area networks (LANs); LAN topologies.

Hafta 2: Multiple access methods used in broadcast networks; metropolitan area networks (MANs), wide area networks (WANs), wireless networks, internetworks; basics of network architecture, layered network structure, interfaces and services, connection-oriented and connectionless services.
Hafta 3: Virtual circuit, reliable and unreliable services, service primitives, OSI reference model. Functions of OSI layers, TCP/IP model; network examples, network standardization.

Hafta 4: Basics of data transmission, information content, transmission rate, channel capacity; data transmission types, serial, parallel, asynchronous and synchronous transmission; data communication services, SMDS, X.25.
Hafta 5: Frame Relay (FR), Integrated Services Digital Networks (ISDN).
Hafta 6: Asynchronous transfer mode (ATM), Broadband ISDN (BISDN).
Hafta 7: ATM service classes, ATM-LAN emulation; physical layer, RS-232, RS-449, X.21, ISDN and V.35 interfaces; types of transmission medium; wireless transmission.
Hafta 8: Functions and services of data link layer, framing methods, flow control methods, stop-and-wait protocol, and sliding window protocol.

Hafta 9: Error control methods; error detection methods, parity bit, LRC, and CRC methods; error correction methods, automatic repeat request-ARQ; stop-and-wait ARQ, go-back-N ARQ, selective reject ARQ; Hamming codes; performance of error correction methods, bit error rate (BER), transfer rate of information bits (TRIB).

Hafta 10: Examples of data link layer protocols (HDLC, SLIP and PPP); relation of network devices to OSI model; repeaters, passive hubs, bridges, spanning tree algorithm, FDDI, LAN switches (Layer-2 switches), routers, types of routing, type of routing algorithms, static routing algorithms.
Hafta 12: Commemoration of Atatürk, Youth and Sports Day
Hafta 14: Tunneling, brouters, layer-3 switches, gateways.
resources: Tanenbaum, A. S. 2003. Computer Networks (4th Edition). Pearson Education Ltd. (Prentice-Hall PTR). ISBN 0-13-066102-3, www.prenhall.com/tanenbaum
B. Demir Öner, Communication Networks Class Note Slides, Blackboard System, 2022.
Öner, D. 2010. Bilgisayar Ağları (2nd Edition). Papatya Yayıncılık. İstanbul. ISBN 978-975-67-97-43-3.

courses.name: Ergonomics
courses.purpose: To deliver background information about the basic principles and applications of Ergonomics.
courses.description: Definition, history, multidisciplinary applications, using anthropometric design and interaction of mechanical, manual and automatic systems of Ergonomics.
learning_outcomes: Identifying the interdisciplinary nature of ergonomics, express the most important elements of man-machine systems
Learning the importance of anthropometric design and how we use anthropometric data in design phase
Understanding physical workload and energy expenditure, individual differences, measurement of physical workload, understanding tracking and handling work-related musculoskeletal disorders (WMSDs)
Learning Choice of Work Posture, Standing, Sitting, or Sit-Standing, Identifying Poor Postures, Repetitive Motion Injury, Guidelines to Minimize Repetitive Motion Injury
Understanding design of controls, designing of work environment, applying principles of ergonomics

Hafta 12: Man-Computer Interaction, How to work with computer?, Visual Fatigue, Reducing Reflections and Glare, Viewing Angle, Viewing Distance
Hafta 13: Designing of Work Environmental Illumination, Noise Shift Work, Vibration, Case Studies

courses.name: Metaheuristics
courses.purpose: This course aims to design metaheuristic algorithms for optimization problems in industrial engineering and general engineering, and to enable students to effectively apply existing and newly designed metaheuristic methods using Python.
courses.description: Recap of mathematical modelling and optimization methods, Travelling Salesman Problem (TSP), foundations of metaheuristics, single-solution-based models such as Simulated Annealing and Tabu Search, population-based models such as Genetic Algorithms and Ant Colony Optimization, and multiobjective metaheuristics.

Model and Solve Optimization Problems Using Metaheuristics
Develop and Apply Single-Solution-Based Metaheuristic Algorithms
Develop and Apply Population-Based Metaheuristic Models
Understanding Multiobjective Metaheuristics for Complex Problem Solving

Hafta 2: Recap of Mathematical Modelling and Optimization Methods -Cont'd

Hafta 12: Population Based Models : Ant Colony Optimization
Hafta 14: Project Final Reports and Presentation

courses.name: Project Management

The course also focuses on the relations between Human Resources Management, Procurement Management, Quality Management, and Project Management.

Project manager's role
Project organization planning and formulation
Data collection and analysis
Preparation of project study report, project feasibility
Using CPM/PERT techniques

Hafta 6: Project Duration Time Shorten, Project Team Management
Hafta 9: Development and Performance Measurement and Evaluation

courses.name: Technology Management

courses.name: Special Topics in Industrial Engineering
courses.purpose: The general aim of this course is to introduce students to the fundamental concepts and modules of SAP ERP, focusing on the integration of MM, SD, and FI processes and their applications in business contexts. In addition, the course seeks to provide an understanding of Artificial Intelligence, machine learning, deep learning, and generative AI, emphasizing their role in organizational workflows. By combining ERP and AI solutions, students will develop the ability to design integrated business processes and demonstrate both theoretical knowledge and practical skills through project work at the end of the semester.
courses.description: This course begins with an introduction to the fundamental concepts and modules of SAP ERP, focusing on the integration of MM, SD, and FI processes. It then explores the role of Artificial Intelligence and Generative AI in business, including machine learning and deep learning methods. Applications of SAP Business AI and the use of SAP Joule for natural language interaction and automation scenarios are examined. Students learn to combine ERP and AI solutions to design integrated business workflows and demonstrate their knowledge through project work at the end of the semester.
learning_outcomes: Be able to identify and explain the fundamental concepts and modules of SAP ERP
Demonstrate understanding of MM, SD, and FI processes and their integration
Apply ERP process integration through practical exercises
Understand the basic principles of Artificial Intelligence and its applications in business contexts
Explain machine learning and deep learning methods with relevant examples
Evaluate how SAP Business AI can be integrated into organizational processes
Utilize SAP Joule for natural language interaction within business workflows
Design automation scenarios supported by Generative AI
Combine ERP and AI solutions to create integrated business workflows
Solve real-world problems using SAP systems in applied scenarios
Present knowledge through group projects and case studies effectively
Discuss the strategic importance of ERP and AI technologies in digital enterprises
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to ERP Systems - Overview of enterprise resource planning and its role in organizations.
Hafta 2: SAP ERP Fundamentals - Core concepts, architecture, and system navigation.
Hafta 3: Materials Management (MM) Module - Procurement processes and inventory management.
Hafta 4: Sales and Distribution (SD) Module - Order management, billing, and customer relations.
Hafta 5: Financial Accounting (FI) Module - General ledger, accounts payable/receivable, and reporting.
Hafta 6: Integration of MM, SD, and FI - Cross-module workflows and data consistency.
Hafta 7: Artificial Intelligence in Business - Principles of AI and its applications in enterprise systems.
Hafta 8: Midterm Exam - Covers ERP fundamentals and module integration.
Hafta 9: Machine Learning and Deep Learning Basics - Key methods and business use cases.
Hafta 10: Generative AI Concepts - Text, image, and process automation in enterprise contexts.
Hafta 11: SAP Business AI Applications - Practical examples of AI-driven enhancements in SAP.
Hafta 12: SAP Joule - Natural language interaction and intelligent automation scenarios.
Hafta 13: Final Project Presentations I - Student groups present integrated ERP + AI workflow projects.
Hafta 14: Final Project Presentations II & Course Review - Remaining presentations and overall synthesis of learning outcomes.

courses.name: Innovation and Entrepreneurship
courses.purpose: This course introduces innovation processes, technology entrepreneurship, and the commercialization of engineering ideas. It aims to help students identify opportunities, develop business models, evaluate feasibility, and launch engineering-based ventures while considering economic, societal, and sustainability factors.
courses.description: The course covers the science-technology-industry triangle, entrepreneurial mindset, methods for identifying new and emerging ideas, technologies and products, innovation management tools, intellectual property rights, and the steps of establishing a startup company. Students also work on questionnaires, portfolio preparation, project selection, business planning, and presentations.
learning_outcomes: To comprehend the concept of science, technology and industry triangle and to relate each corners of triangles
To describe entrepreneurial mind-set and its value in cutting-edge, fast-moving technologically competitive world
To employ methodology for data collection for identifying new and emerging ideas, technologies and products
To examine usage of innovation management tools

courses.name: Applied Quantum Physics
courses.purpose: The main purpose of this course is to get science and engineering students familiar with quantum mechanics and to show them the basic postulates of quantum theory. courses.description: This course covers photons and electrons, the physical meaning of quantum wave functions, quantum measurements and the uncertainty principle, point particles in quantum wells, potential barriers and tunneling, electron spin, identical particle systems, entanglement, atomic and molecular structures, the Kronig-Penney model of crystalline solids, electronic energy bands, semi-conductors and lasers, and atomic nuclei and radioactive decays.
learning_outcomes: They will know the Schrödinger equation.
They will learn the fundamental principles of quantum mechanics.
They will learn some of the most important applications of quantum mechanics.
weekly_topics: Hafta 4: Bohr model of a H-atom.
Hafta 6: Plane waves for free particles. Infinite potential well problem in 1-dimension.
Hafta 7: Potential barriers. Quantum tunnel effect.
Hafta 8: Stern-Gerlach experiment and the electron spin. Exclusion principle.
Hafta 9: Identical particles in a potential well. Fermi energy level. Entangled 2-spin states.
Hafta 11: Quantum theory of solids. Types of molecular bonding: Ionic, covalent and H-bonds.
Hafta 12: Energy band structure of crystalline solids. Conductors and insulators. Electron holes. Impurities.
Hafta 13: Semi-conductor devices: Diodes and transistors.
resources: University Physics with Modern Physics, H.D. Young and R.A. Freedman (Pearson, 14th Edition, 2016), Chapters 38-43

courses.name: Cyber Security
courses.purpose: The primary objective of this course is to provide students with a solid foundation in computer system security and digital forensics. Students will learn how to identify cyber threats and system vulnerabilities, understand how attacks are carried out, and explore various strategies for preventing and mitigating such attacks. The course also emphasizes practical skills, including the use of forensic tools and software such as Autopsy, Nmap, Zenmap, Wireshark, and Magnet. By the end of the course, students will be equipped with the knowledge and technical abilities to analyze cyber incidents and conduct digital investigations in real-world scenarios.
courses.description: This course provides a comprehensive introduction to the fundamental principles of computer system security and digital forensics. It focuses on identifying threats and vulnerabilities in computer systems and examines strategies for reducing these risks. Students learn how hackers identify victims, carry out attacks, and use methods to gain unauthorized access to systems. The course also includes practical work with software tools commonly used in cyber security and digital forensics.
learning_outcomes: Differentiate between digital forensics and forensic medicine.
Explain concepts of computer system security, digital forensics, and information security.
Conduct investigations on computers involved in cybercrimes.
Work with hash tables, hex tables, Exif data, and use ProDiscover software.
Identify vulnerabilities in network systems and analyze countermeasures.
Describe how cyber-attacks are carried out and explain defense techniques against them.
Use digital forensics and security tools such as Autopsy, Nmap, Zenmap, Wireshark, and Magnet.
Distinguish between number systems and apply conversions in digital investigations.
Identify and explain the functions of hardware components in a network system.
Demonstrate familiarity with cryptographic concepts and encryption techniques.
Apply steganography techniques using tools like GIMP.
Analyze the use of biometric systems in forensic applications.
Understand and apply ethical principles in digital forensics investigations.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Course syllabus description, Introduction to computer systems security and cyber security.
Hafta 2: The concept of computer forensics.
Hafta 3: Viruses, Cyber-attack detection on computers.
Hafta 4: Numbering systems and their usage in digital forensics,
Hafta 6: Use of Exif and ProDiscover software.
Hafta 12: Stenography and use of GIMP.

Notice an improvement in their ability to write fiction that is more sophisticated, compelling and dynamic

courses.name: Archaeology
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce archaeology as a science that investigates, documents, and interprets the material remains of past communities and to help students assimilate the concept of cultural heritage. It also aims to provide an understanding of the historical development of archaeology in Turkey within the framework of scientific studies and relevant legal regulations.
courses.description: The course covers the development of archaeology in the world and in Turkey, the basic concepts used in archaeology, the relationship between archaeology and other disciplines, the methods of archaeological excavations and surveys, legal legislation, types of finds, documentation methods and resources, and archaeology and museology. It also includes the chronology of Anatolia and Mesopotamia from the first people to the present, as well as important archaeological research and discoveries in Turkey and around the world.
learning_outcomes: Knows the scope and method of archaeology as a branch of science.
Understands and interprets the terminology related to archaeology and its relationship with other disciplines.
Knows the historical development process of archaeology in the world and in Turkey.
Has a command of the legal legislation related to archaeological excavations and surveys.
Understands the importance of archaeological remains in the light of the concepts of cultural property and cultural heritage and knows the necessary methods and processes for their protection.
Has knowledge about the life styles, administrative and political structures, architectural and cultural activities, daily lives, historical geography and economic lives of past civilisations.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Course Introduction and Introduction to Basic Concepts
Hafta 2: Development Process of Archaeology in the World and Turkey
Hafta 3: Basic Concepts Used in Archaeology
Hafta 4: The Relationship of Archeology with Other Branches of Science.
Hafta 5: Methodology of Archaeological Excavations and Surveys and Legal Legislation
Hafta 6: Find Types, Documentation Methods and Resources in Archaeology
Hafta 8: From the First People to the Present: Chronology of Anatolia and Mesopotamia
Hafta 10: Archaeological Research in Turkey 1: Prehistoric Archaeology
Hafta 11: Archaeological Research in Turkey 2: Hittite, Urartu, Phrygian and Lydian Archaeology
Hafta 12: Archaeological Research in Turkey 3: Ancient Greek and Roman Archaeology
Hafta 13: Archaeological Researches in Turkey 4: Archaeological Researches of Other Periods
Hafta 14: Important Archaeological Researches and Discoveries in the World

courses.name: Biodiversity
courses.purpose: This course is designed to teach students the concept of biodiversity with all aspects, the consequences of the loss of biodiversity, the importance of biodiversity and related studies about the conservation of biodiversity in Turkey and the World. The main objectives of the course are to teach students the concept of biodiversity and to make them aware of the elements of biodiversity, to increase students' awareness about the importance of biodiversity on a global scale and for our country, and to enable students understand the importance of biological diversity for people's future.
courses.description: The subjects of this course are definition of biodiversity, factors affecting biodiversity, species diversity and endemic species, genetic diversity and ecosystem diversity, importance and protection of biodiversity, faunistic and floristic appearance and geographic distribution throughout the World, Turkey biodiversity and comparison with the neighboring countries, protection of natural areas (national parks and nature conservation areas) in terms of biodiversity, factors threatening biodiversity, threats on genetic diversity and discussion of solutions on a global scale.
learning_outcomes: Learn the basic concepts of biodiversity.
Understand species diversity and endemic species, genetic diversity and ecosystem diversity, importance and conservation of biodiversity.
Learn the importance of conservation of natural areas (national parks and nature conservation areas) in terms of biodiversity.
Learn the factors that threaten biodiversity, the threats on genetic diversity and proposes solutions on a global scale.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to biodiversity: Definition of biodiversity, factors affecting biodiversity, basic concepts related to biodiversity
Hafta 2: Species diversity and endemic species, genetic diversity
Hafta 4: Importance and protection of biodiversity
Hafta 5: Faunistic and floristic status worldwide and geographical distribution
Hafta 6: Turkey biodiversity and comparison with the neighboring countries
Hafta 8: Important areas in the world in terms of biodiversity
Hafta 9: Protection of natural areas (national parks and nature conservation areas)
Hafta 13: Threats on biodiversity and genetic diversity and solutions on a global scale

courses.name: Ecology
courses.purpose: This course is designed to teach students the basic concepts and scope of environmental science, which is of great importance in terms of present global problems. The course aims to inform students about environmental science and basic concepts, ecosystem dynamics, anthropogenic effects, biodiversity, conservation areas, and ecological problems and solutions.
courses.description: Basic concepts of ecology, ecosystem and ecological factors, material and energy relations in ecosystems, interrelationships between living things and food chains, ecological cycles and anthropogenic effects, energy resources, changes in ecosystems, ecosystems and natural conservation areas, population ecology, ecological effects of environmental pollution, ecological problems and solutions.
learning_outcomes: Learn environmental science and basic concepts.
Learn ecosystem and ecological factor.
Understand environmental components and its influences on the lives of living things.
Know the natural resource values and learn its ecological importance.
Get information basic principles for producing modern solutions to environmental problems.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction of Ecology: The definition of ecology, the current ecological events, the criteria of ecology research, basic rules and principles of ecology
Hafta 2: Basic concepts of ecology: The environment, environmental pollution, biotope, ecological niche, biosphere, habitat, ecological balance, flora, fauna, and etc
Hafta 3: Ecosystem: Definition and classification of ecosystem, structure and elements of ecosystem, functions of ecosystem
Hafta 4: Progressive factors of ecosystems, the rules of environmental factors, ecological factors; biotic and abiotic factors
Hafta 5: Material and energy relations in ecosystem: Concepts of energy and substance, production in ecosystems, energy flow, evaluation of thermodynamic laws for ecosystems
Hafta 6: Foods chains, food pyramids, food network, biological accumulation
Hafta 8: Ecological cycles: Substance cycles (Hydrological and biogeochemical cycle), carbon, nitrogen, phosphorus, sulfur cycles, other cycles, energy resources
Hafta 9: Anthropogenic effects on ecological cycles and results: Global climate changes, acid rain, ozone depletion, the eutrophication, radioactive pollution, the protection of ecological cycles
Hafta 10: Natural changes of ecosystems in time: short-term, long-term and sequential changes due to periodic events, ecological succession, changes in ecosystems and human impact on ecosystems

Hafta 11: Earth's great ecosystems and conservation areas: Aquatic and terrestrial ecosystems, nature conservation areas and criteria for identification, importance of nature conservation, nature conservation area in Turkey and World
Hafta 12: Population ecology: Definition of population, related features and concepts of populations, development of populations and controlling factors, results of applications of population ecology
Hafta 13: Ecological effects of environmental pollution, behavior of pollutants in ecosystem, monitoring of pollution and ecological change, ecological problems and solutions

courses.name: Istanbul in The Movies
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to recognize and examine representations of the city in cinema through film language by watching films from Turkish and world cinema that were shot in Istanbul or use Istanbul as a setting. By learning cinematic terminology, students will distinguish differences in representation across genres, read the socio-cultural and geographical transformation of Istanbul past and present through films, and comparatively analyze how Istanbul is positioned in international cinema.
courses.description: Throughout the course, the relationship between urban space and cinema will be examined through the history of cinema. The development and characteristics of the modern city will be presented on the basis of early examples of film art, and Istanbul's spatial contributions to cinema will be highlighted. In the following weeks, Istanbul's place in Turkish and world cinema as both a setting and a character will be explored through selected films, while also providing students with a comparative environment for discussion.
learning_outcomes: Explain the city-cinema relationship from a historical perspective and locate Istanbul within major periods of film history.
Use core film concepts to conduct basic scene/sequence analysis.
Interpret Istanbul's significance in Turkish cinema by relating filmic representations to migration, class, gender, memory, and urbanization-urban transformation.
Interprets the importance of Istanbul for the World cinema through sample movies.
Compare Istanbul's representations in world cinema through the lens of "inside perspective vs. outside gaze," and evaluate stereotypes/orientalist framings using selected scenes.

Hafta 3: Development of Cinema / Development of Modern City
Hafta 4: The function and importance of urban space for the art of cinema
Hafta 5: The relationship between sinema and İstanbul / Place
Hafta 6: The relationship between sinema and İstanbul / Character
Hafta 8: The importance of İstanbul in Turkish Cinema
Hafta 12: The Changing Function of İstanbul in World Cinema

courses.name: Intercultural Communication
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to classify, evaluate and discuss the theoretical foundations and development of the discipline of intercultural communication as a field of study, different research traditions and approaches. In this context, the definitions of culture will be reviewed and examined in all aspects and communication problems arising from cultural differences will be emphasized. At lesson; based on the definitions of culture and communication, the importance of intercultural communication, its historical process and the importance of cultural differences as a result of globalization will be examined.
courses.description: In this course, the relationship between culture and communication, cultural differentiation and the importance of intercultural communication for individuals and organizations in the global world will be discussed. Within the scope of the course, students will have the opportunity to understand cultural behaviors, communication barriers, cultural adaptation processes, intercultural communication competence and cultural differences. Based on the definitions of culture and communication, the definitions of intercultural communication, its importance and historical process are examined. The importance of cultural differences is emphasized within the framework of globalization. Among the theorists, Geert Hofstede's Theory of Cultural Dimensions and Edward T. Hall's view of the phenomenon of culture are covered.
learning_outcomes: Learn the definitions of culture and communication.
Will be able to evaluate the concept of intercultural communication from multiple perspectives.
Examines the historical development of intercultural communication.
Understands the importance of intercultural communication.
Will be able to explain the characteristics of intercultural differentiation and organizational behavior.
Examines intercultural communication theories.
Will be able to classify the factors affecting intercultural communication.
Learns to take into account different cultural characteristics.
Interpret the reflections of cultural differences in different disciplines.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Overview of general introduction: Definitions of culture and communication
Hafta 3: Society, social life, social norms
Hafta 4: Importance of intercultural communication: Brief overview of history, approaches
Hafta 6: Culture shock, intercultural sensitivity and intercultural harmony
Hafta 8: Edward Hall Information Systems Theory
Hafta 9: Hofstede's Theory of Cultural Dimensions
Hafta 10: Intercultural communication processes: Verbal, nonverbal and visual communication

courses.name: Global Warming and Climate Change
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to adopt a learner-centered approach to examining the scientific basis, causes, and consequences of global warming and climate change, and to provide students with fundamental knowledge of the climate system, greenhouse gases, and the environmental, social, and economic impacts of climate change. The course also aims to raise students awareness of climate change mitigation and adaptation approaches, introduce national and international climate policies, and promote sustainable living.
courses.description: This course covers the scientific foundations of global warming and climate change, the climate system and greenhouse effect, greenhouse gases and the carbon cycle, causes of climate change, environmental, social, and economic impacts, climate change mitigation and adaptation approaches, national and international climate policies, sustainable living, and individual and institutional responsibilities.
learning_outcomes: Explain the basic concepts, processes, and scientific foundations of global warming and climate change.
Describe the relationships between the climate system, greenhouse gases, and the carbon cycle.
Evaluate the environmental, social, and economic impacts of climate change.
Distinguish between human-induced and natural drivers of climate change.
Compare climate change mitigation and adaptation strategies.
Identify the main objectives and policy instruments of national and international climate policies.
Demonstrate awareness of individual and societal responsibilities in addressing climate change at both daily life and institutional levels.

Hafta 4: Causes of climate change, Fossil fuel use, Land-use changes
Hafta 5: Environmental impacts of climate change, Ecosystems, Biodiversity
Hafta 6: İklim değişikliğinin sağlık üzerindeki etkileri, Aşırı hava olayları, Riskler

Hafta 9: Climate change mitigation strategies, Mitigation approaches
Hafta 10: Climate change adaptation, Adaptation strategies, Vulnerability
Hafta 11: International climate policies, United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change, Paris Agreement
Hafta 12: National climate policies, Climate change policies of Turkey
Hafta 13: Individual and institutional approaches to climate action, Sustainable living

courses.name: Artificial Intelligence Literacy
courses.purpose: This course examines and evaluates the definition, history, types, and components of artificial intelligence along with big data and artificial intelligence applications in social, ethical, academic, and professional contexts. In the context of application, the aim is to provide students with general information about the heterogeneous structure of artificial intelligence and the different methodologies that constitute it. The reflection of artificial intelligence on daily life and academic/professional fields will be assessed with examples. The effects of ethical discussions and correct usage methodology on generative artificial intelligence applications will be evaluated.
courses.description: The aim is to provide students with general information about the heterogeneous structure of artificial intelligence in academic, professional, social and ethical contexts and the differences that constitute it (types, methodologies), and to evaluate artificial intelligence applications. The reflection of ethical debates and professional transformation on artificial intelligence applications will be evaluated with examples. This course will use current artificial intelligence applications and case studies to explain and understand the technological transformation taking place, focusing on the definition of artificial intelligence, its relationship with big data, and ethical issues. By establishing a connection between technology and everyday life, the aim is for students to gain competence in this field and think innovatively through the hands-on experience of generative artificial intelligence tools. It will explain how a simple command (prompt) or data is used by generative artificial intelligence to transform it into valuable output (text, visual, code) and provide an opportunity to use these tools first-hand. Fundamental concepts that have left their mark on the development of artificial intelligence and applications that have had a universal impact will be explained through practical applications, current case studies, written and material resources, equipping students with digital literacy (artificial intelligence literacy) and encouraging them to think critically.
learning_outcomes: Get to know the culture of living with Artificial Intelligence

All faculty and college students learn to follow current technological innovations in a general context and to understand the relationships between technological, social, ethical, academic, and professional fields and the connections between these fields through artificial intelligence applications.
It evaluates the relevant fields (affected by artificial intelligence) from different perspectives (interdisciplinary). It critically assesses the knowledge and skills acquired in this field.

It identifies the national and international issues brought about by artificial intelligence (e.g., algorithmic bias, workforce transformation, disinformation) and develops solutions based on responsible use principles.
It fosters social responsibility awareness and gains in the field of universal values and artificial intelligence ethics.

courses.name: Hollywood; Treasure of Legends

It aims to provide students with a broad interdisciplinary perspective on Hollywood history, political thought, legal events, human rights, and the relationship between art and popular culture.
courses.description: The course covers the heterogeneous structure of Hollywood and its differences, the reflection of political ideas and legal events on films and music, the effects of World War II and the Cold War, and the lives and works of legendary artists. It also introduces social heritage objects associated with legendary figures and evaluates social issues, ethics, morality, race, gender discrimination, poverty, environment, and rights-seeking through films, music, anecdotes, and visual and written materials.
learning_outcomes: Students learn about economic, social, cultural, historical, political, legal etc. areas and the relationships between them through movies and anecdotes.

Students learn about political history, the effects of government and policies, and social issues, and gain a different perspective.
Learns the value of items used by legendary characters over time and the process by which they become priceless.
Cultural gains related to universal values are achieved.

courses.name: Food as a Theme in Fine Arts
courses.purpose: This course aims to raise awareness about the importance of traditional production methods in the food and beverage culture to be passed on to future generations. It also encourages students to research local cuisine, recover nearly forgotten flavors, and understand the cultural value of traditional dishes through oral history and exhibition-based presentation.
courses.description: The course examines studies, interviews, and documentary works on the importance and continuity of traditional cuisine culture in Turkish and world cuisine. Historical documents, novels, poems, articles, and research are used as guiding materials. Students research local cuisine as a continuation of traditional food culture and reproduce a traditional dish, which is then presented as a tasting exhibition.
learning_outcomes: Knowing about the value of the traditional
Researching local food culture
Reproducing a traditional dish
Conducting a memoir interview about the local food
Presenting the traditional local food produced within the scope of an exhibition
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Seed, Viktor Ananias, Roots of Local Food, Tatuta, Wwoof, Permaculture
Hafta 2: Food and Drink in Science Fiction
Hafta 3: Evliya Çelebi, Broquière and Ibn Battuta's Food Culture in Travelogues
Hafta 4: Literature and Food. Jean Anthelme Brillat-Savarin, Physiology of Flavor. Food Figure in Kaygusuz Abdal's Semati.
Hafta 5: Food Art. El Bulli Foundation. Molecular Gastronomy.
Hafta 14: Our lost tastes: Armenian and Greek cuisine in literature and cinema

courses.name: Food and Sustainability
courses.purpose: This course focuses on effects of contemporary global food production and consumption patterns and practices on the environment. It explores the evolution of the contemporary food regime and various movements around the world to develop more sustainable alternatives.
courses.description: Within the scope of the course, information will be given about sustainability, sustainable food, agricultural practices, sustainable gastronomy, eco-gastronomy, geographical marking and identification, sustainable food movements, sustainable nutrition models and consumer behaviours.
learning_outcomes: Students learn the concept of sustainability and understand its importance in the food industry.
Gains information of food safety.
Have knowledge about sustainable agriculture, organic farming, good agricultural practices.
Gains knowledge about sustainable gastronomy and eco-gastronomy.
Gains knowledge about geographic marking and gastronomic identity issues and learns developments.
Gains knowledge about sustainable food movements.

Hafta 2: Sustainable food, omponents of sustainable food systems
Hafta 3: Sustainable agriculture, organic farming, good agricultural practices

Hafta 6: Geographical marking and gastronomic identity within the scope of sustainable gastronomy
