courses.name: Drawing
courses.purpose: It aims to understand the importance of the pattern in plastic arts and to improve the visual perception and memory of the student. Its primary goal is to teach plastic elements such as line, contour, rhythm, perspective, proportion, balance, composition, light-shadow and toning.
courses.description: Pattern lesson is based on object (living model, organic or inorganic object) inquiry. Plastic elements and principles such as line, contour, form, rhythm, perspective, proportion, balance, composition, light-shadow and toning are applied by drawing on an established composition. Students gain the ability to depict what they see and perceive from an artistic perspective.
learning_outcomes: To be able to draw all the forms around with analytical approach.

To be able to show the composition in which the object (organic or inorganic object, living model) is in the space, taking into account the values of proportion, proportion, light and dark, on a two-dimensional surface with analytical analysis method.
Understanding the model or object in space
Ability to think, perceive and design multidimensionally necessary to produce original work and design improves his knowledge and skill

Hafta 8: Human figure dimensioning, proportion-proportion and painting.

courses.name: Experimental Design II
courses.purpose: Students can produce unique, creative and bold jobs that break the template thoughts in their heads and push the boundaries.
courses.description: In this course, we will try to lay the foundations of experimental typography and look for the visual expression of typography with symbolic simplification. In the conceptual approach, the most basic and plain element of graphic design will be found in the ways of creative use of the point and the texture, which has an important place in graphic design too. During all these studies, the use of negative space will be taken into account. The presentation of all the work done is done by the students.
learning_outcomes: To gaint a different perspective on projects in design.
Gaining the ability to push the limits of principles with the awareness of the basic principles of design in graphic design projects.
To gain the ability to educate the eye to provide vision in an abstract and simple way.
To gain the ability to provide evaluation in terms of form and texture.
To gain the ability to make different arrangements with the same elements.

courses.name: Children and Youth Theatre Practices
courses.purpose: The main aim of this course is to enable students to become competent in children's and youth theatre and to realize that this field is a special specialty that is often underestimated. To achieve this goal, writing, adaptation, and staging practices are emphasized.
courses.description: Various practices are carried out with students to explore the essentials of children's and youth theatre. Anti-authoritarian and anti-hierarchical children's/youth literature texts, cinema, and theatre plays are discussed in class, and students are encouraged to create their own texts and plays. The course includes rewriting exercises to reveal discriminatory elements in traditional fairy tales, short play writing based on personal lives, and adapting emancipatory children's literature to the stage.
learning_outcomes: Recognize the thoughts and feelings of children and young people.
Engage with liberating children's literature.
Develop skills in writing or rewriting original texts for children and youths.
Contribute to children's and youth theatre projects in ways that improve the quality of the project.
Critically approach the memorization and knowledge of children and youth and question their taboos.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Let's remember the previous semester: Who is a child, what are basic principles of children's theater? How should a Children's Theater be?
Hafta 2: Discussing the proposition "Childhood and youth is not a biological category, but a social phenomenon" with the data of sociologists.
Hafta 3: Analysis of the "Human Age" adaptation of the Grips Theater "Gök Toprak Hava Deniz" play by Erasalan Sağlam
Hafta 4: Rewriting Examples of Fairy Tales: Başka Bir Kırmızı Başlıklı Kız Kül Prensi Bitli Rapunzel Vejetaryen Külkedisi
Hafta 5: Evaluation of LGBTI friendly books

Hafta 6: How can the texts of Sandworm, Stone Steps, Cecü's Earth Dwarfs, My Body Belong to Me, Red Tree, Mysterious Land Terijan be a source of inspiration when producing for children and young people?
Hafta 8: Watching and analyzing Ma vie de Courgette movie
Hafta 9: Rewriting of Grimms and Andersen fairy tales.
Hafta 10: Touching dogmas in the adult mind (Writing short texts on love, sexuality, death, war, violence, peer bullying)
Hafta 11: How our childhood and adolescence can inspire us to write, adapt and stage.
Hafta 12: Staging a short play using digital tools
Hafta 13: Discussion of the plays "Written, Adapted or staged" in the classroom I
